title: Test device bearer production control boundary

claims.statement: The protocol device credential lifecycle can freshly authorize one bearer against an exact Show, scope, Preview or Program target, and control identifier without resolving a Studio user or session.

The production service can execute one explicit component visibility intent with expected-revision concurrency, operation-id idempotency, Preview isolation, and externally supplied direct-Program authorization, but no device HTTP route currently composes these authorities.

HTTP bearer usage standardizes the Authorization header, a 401 challenge for missing or invalid credentials, and a 403 response when valid credentials are insufficient; bearer values in URLs risk disclosure through logs and history.

H-010: A dedicated device route mounted outside interactive-session authentication can accept a bearer only from the Authorization header, derive Show, target, and control authority from route coordinates, freshly authorize that exact action on every request, and then execute one revision-bound visibility intent without inheriting Studio identity or accepting client-supplied authority.

Review accepted H-010 only for the dedicated HTTP visibility-intent boundary after credential transport, Studio-principal separation, exact fresh authorization, Show state, authority substitution, Preview and Program isolation, rotation, revocation, expiration, tampering, idempotency, concurrency, bounded error and receipt, local three-second response-budget, repository-quality, build, and audit evidence passed.
claims.evidence: server focused tests, ADR-0014, and Human Doctrine review on 2026-07-21

claims.statement: This proposed Slice does not establish device action discovery, credential listing or lost-response recovery, cues, Take, control-value mutation, feedback transport, hardware or Companion integration, per-device rate policy, audit retention, renderer observation, multi-process behavior, or linearizable revocation during an already-authorized in-flight mutation.

successCriteria.statement: A device visibility request accepts exactly one syntactically valid Bearer credential from Authorization and rejects missing, malformed, query, body, cookie, or ambiguous interactive-session credential transport before production mutation.
successCriteria.verification: Focused HTTP transport and no-mutation tests.

successCriteria.statement: Every request freshly authorizes the route-derived Show, target, component control identifier, and component.visibility:write scope; invalid bearers return a bounded 401 challenge and insufficient authority returns bounded 403 without credential details.
successCriteria.verification: HTTP tests for wrong Show, target, control, scope, expiration, rotation, revocation, and token tampering.

successCriteria.statement: The body can provide only visible, operationId, and expectedRevision intent data; changing body fields cannot substitute the route-derived Show, target, component, scope, or direct-Program authority.
An authorized Preview request changes only Preview, while a Program request mutates Program only when the freshly resolved credential explicitly grants Program for the exact control.

The route preserves production expected-revision conflicts, operation-id replay, operation-id conflict, missing-component failure, and returns a bounded server-state receipt within three seconds under local in-process HTTP test conditions without claiming network, renderer, or device observation.
successCriteria.verification: Focused HTTP concurrency, idempotency, failure, receipt, and local response-budget tests.

successCriteria.statement: Studio session cookies alone cannot call the device route, device bearers do not create or resolve a Studio principal, and mixed Studio-cookie plus device-bearer requests are rejected as ambiguous authority.
successCriteria.verification: HTTP principal-separation and injected-auth call-count tests.

definitionOfDone.statement: CHG-0019 records facts, H-010, the accepted inference, measurable criteria, and unresolved guarantees for the demonstrated device HTTP visibility boundary.

The Slice changes only device HTTP authentication and visibility-intent composition, runtime typing, focused tests, and proposed governance records; it adds no feedback socket, action catalog, Studio UI, or hardware adapter.

Review attempts to refute H-010 across credential transport, principal separation, fresh exact authorization, authority substitution, Program capability, Show state, idempotency, concurrency, and bounded error disclosure.
Governance, focused server checks, full repository checks, build, and audit pass for the accepted Slice manifest.
definitionOfDone.evidence: npm run governance:check, server checks, npm run check, npm run build, and npm audit

definitionOfDone.statement: ADR-0014 was created only after the human authority accepted H-010 within the demonstrated HTTP visibility-intent boundary.
definitionOfDone.evidence: ADR-0014 context and Human Doctrine review on 2026-07-21
